commit_scope: Streaming file I/O for fkwu, end to end: replace the buffered (truncating, double-copied) read path with host-native streaming primitives in the emitted walker, expose them through a BML high-grammar interface, and lift the proof orchestration's DECISION onto the form-native shell. (1) hati-os-kernel-emit.fk: read_file_slice/read_file (tags 62/63) and write_file_text (tag 104) stream directly through the string pool with dynamic resize instead of a fixed 65536-byte buffer; new streaming carriers file_open/file_read/file_close (tags 133/134/135 — free above main's fs_list op 132) with explicit fkc-flat arity rows. (2) form-flatten.fk: register the three ops + mark them effectful. (3) source-compiler.fk: fsc-strip-bml-comments lifts the BML `//` doc-comment stripping into the compiler itself, so .bml modules lower flattener-clean with no downstream sed. (4) file-io.bml: the interface as a generic class with block-bodied recursive higher-order methods (fold-chunks/fold-lines), a reader-handle object, and a cooperative Task async surface. (5) file-io-shell.fk + tests/file-io-shell-band.fk: the proof orchestration as a Form-shell cell whose witness==255 verdict is the native test/echo gate (decision in Form, not bash); file-io-bml-proof.sh now decides via fio-verdict-code on fkwu and only dispatches on the answer.
evidence_refs: Form-shell orchestration band crosses FOUR-WAY: cd form && ./validate.sh (core + shell prelude chain + file-io-shell.fk + tests/file-io-shell-band.fk) -> '...file-io-shell-band.fk -> 255' and 'fourth arm: 1 band(s) four-way (fkwu + pre-flattened tables)' / '1 ok, 0 divergent — kernels agree on every sample.' The band proves fsh cell lowering, the native test/echo verdict on both PASS (255) and FAIL (7) branches as string and printable-int projections, the passthrough labeling of bootstrap steps (fkc-emit-bml/fkwu-run), and the append redirect.

BML file-I/O interface end-to-end on fkwu: cd form && ./scripts/file-io-bml-proof.sh -> 'fkwu witness: 255 (expected 255)' then 'form-shell verdict (native test gate on fkwu): 1 (1=PASS 0=FAIL)' then PASS. Drives a 256 KiB / 8192-line file through all eight doors (read-all, fold-chunks, fold-lines, read-lines, reader-handle object, read-all-async, read-lines-async, generic descriptor). The verdict's 255 constant + comparison execute on the 4th kernel via the shell's native `test` builtin; bash only dispatches on the int.

fkwu-only by design (honest floor, NOT a divergence): the streaming carriers file_open/file_read/file_close (tags 133/134/135) and the input/host-io path are fkwu host-io carriers — the Go/Rust/TS walkers do not carry this op family, so the bml-proof band is 3-kernel only — unsupported op: host-io file streaming. The DECISION logic (file-io-shell) carries no host-io and therefore crosses four-way; only the host-io carriers themselves are fkwu-native.

No regression in the source-compiler path the `//`-strip lift touches: cd form && ./scripts/storage-port-carriers-test.sh -> 'verdict: 111111' / 'storage port: PASS — one test, identical verdict across memory / file / Postgres.' (exercises form-source-compile-file across memory/file/Postgres carriers).

Root causes resolved during the arc: tag collisions (70/71/72 were and/or/not; then 127/128/129 were claimed by main's kernel_stat + framebuffer subsystem; then 132 by main's fs_list) -> file ops land on 133/134/135, free above the latest main; fkc-flat fell through to a binary handler for unary ops -> explicit fkc-un2/fkc-bin2 arity rows; BML `//` header copied verbatim into lowered Form derailed the flattener's `;`-only comment scan -> lifted into the compiler as fsc-strip-bml-comments. The shell-band fourth=3 seen mid-arc was a stale fkwu/fourth cache from an older base, cleared by rebuilding bin-go and the fourth cache on the current main; live-room-shell and file-io-shell both then crossed four-way.

commands: cd form && ./validate.sh <shell prelude chain> form-stdlib/file-io-shell.fk form-stdlib/tests/file-io-shell-band.fk -> 255
cd form && ./scripts/file-io-bml-proof.sh -> fkwu witness 255, form-shell verdict 1, PASS
fourth_arm_outputs: fourth arm: 1 band(s) four-way (fkwu + pre-flattened tables) — 1 ok, 0 divergent (file-io-shell band, verdict 255)
bml-proof / streaming carriers: 3-kernel only — unsupported op: host-io file streaming (file_open/file_read/file_close are fkwu host-io carriers, by design); proven fkwu-native at witness 255

ci_validation.notes: CI re-runs validate.sh across the kernels including the new file-io-shell four-way band; the host-io streaming carriers run fkwu-native (3-kernel-only by op family, named).

deploy_validation.notes: Form kernel / stdlib change — no api/web public surface, no Hostinger deploy. Runtime proof is the four-way band + the fkwu-native bml proof; deploy stays pending as not-applicable for a form-kernel change.

phase_gate.reason: Streaming I/O lands with host-native carriers, a BML high-grammar interface, the compiler self-stripping `//` preludes, and the proof's decision on the form shell — all proven locally (four-way for the shell verdict band; fkwu-native at 255 for the host-io carriers). CI re-proof and merge are pending. The named next rung is executing passthrough natively (the driver-organ execvp) so the harness reaches the toolchain-free standard receipt.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: Form code reading/writing files on fkwu no longer truncates at 65536 bytes or double-copies through a fixed host buffer; it streams through the string pool. A BML high-grammar interface (generic class, reader-handle object, streaming folds, cooperative Task async) is the door other Form code uses. .bml modules lower flattener-clean (compiler strips `//` preludes). The proof harness's pass/fail decision is computed by the form shell on the 4th kernel, not by a bash string compare.
public_endpoints: none — form-kernel/stdlib change, no public HTTP surface
test_flows: 256 KiB / 8192-line file driven through all eight interface doors on fkwu -> witness 255; native test gate renders PASS (1).
